frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: classic RL model components serve as the interpretable scaffold being augmented

frameworks.name: artificial neural networks
frameworks.one_line: ANNs replace individual RL components one-by-one to increase flexibility

frameworks.name: hybrid cognitive modeling
frameworks.one_line: combining ANN expressivity with classic-model interpretability for human behavior

core_question: Can hybrid ANN/classic models match neural-network fit while retaining interpretability of cognitive mechanisms in human reward-based learning?
key_assumptions: classic RL components can be modularly swapped with ANN substitutes
interpretability is preserved when ANN replaces a single component at a time
human reward-based learning has identifiable mechanistic structure
manipulations: which RL component is replaced by an ANN (one-by-one)
comparison of fully-general ANNs vs hybrid models vs classic RL models
key_findings: hybrid models fit data comparably to fully-general ANNs
hybrid models recover reward-based learning mechanisms similar to classic RL
hybrid models reveal additional reward-blind mechanisms not in classic RL
specific memory contents are identified for reward-based and reward-blind processes
interpretations: ANN/classic hybrids can preserve interpretability while improving predictive power
human learning involves both reward-based and reward-blind mechanisms with distinct memory contents
